title: Uvifera jurgensenii
intro: Uvifera jurgensenii, commonly known as the Jobillo tree or Jatoba, is a tropical plant species native to Central America and Mexico. The bark and leaves of Uvifera jurgensenii have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Uvifera jurgensenii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Uvifera jurgensenii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
